title: centaurea pannonica subsp. substituta
intro: Centaurea pannonica subsp. substituta is a plant species commonly known as Pannonian knapweed or Hungarian knapweed, which belongs to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Centaurea pannonica subsp. substituta include its potential to treat various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and digestive issues, due to its bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant can be used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
